macro.title: How is the housing market weathering war-time economics?
macro.what: War-time economics have sent gas prices skyrocketing and pushed mortgage rates higher over the last five weeks, from a low of 5.99% to a high of 6.64%.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: So far, 2026 job growth is better than 2025
macro.what: March payrolls rose 178,000 and unemployment held at 4.3%, supporting a Fed on hold amid elevated inflation and Iran conflict risk.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Do private listings distort mortgage risk data?
housing.what: Suppressed pre-marketing data can make homes appear to sell faster and higher, influencing comps, appraisals, and AVM inputs.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Thad Wong: The market spoke, Zillow listened. Will the rest of the industry?
housing.what: In this op-ed, Thad Wong notes that Zillow changed its pre-market listings policy as agents and sellers pushed for phased marketing and broader consumer visibility.
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Presupposition is the mistake:  Why housing marketing doesn’t meet the bar
housing.what: Though I’ve never taken a marketing class, I remember fellow students quoting the famous Philip Kotler saying that marketing is “the creation of demand.” Simplistic to be sure, but a useful shorthand for that many-splendored thing that many of us do for a living
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.
